verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचबाणस्य तनयामूषामुपयेमे यदूत्तम: ।तत्र युद्धमभूद् घोरं हरिशङ्करयोर्महत् ।एतत् सर्वं महायोगिन् समाख्यातुं त्वमर्हसि ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācabāṇasya tanayām ūṣāmupayeme yadūttamaḥtatra yuddham abhūd ghoraṁhari-śaṅkarayor mahatetat sarvaṁ mahā-yoginsamākhyātuṁ tvam arhasi
verses.text: King Parīkṣit said: The best of the Yadus married Bāṇāsura’s daughter, Ūṣā, and as a result a great, fearsome battle occurred between Lord Hari and Lord Śaṅkara. Please explain everything about this incident, O most powerful of mystics.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचबाण: पुत्रशतज्येष्ठो बलेरासीन्महात्मन: ।येन वामनरूपाय हरयेऽदायि मेदिनी ॥तस्यौरस: सुतो बाण: शिवभक्तिरत: सदा ।मान्यो वदान्यो धीमांश्च सत्यसन्धो द‍ृढव्रत: ।शोणिताख्ये पुरे रम्ये स राज्यमकरोत् पुरा ॥तस्य शम्भो: प्रासादेन किङ्करा इव तेऽमरा: ।सहस्रबाहुर्वाद्येन ताण्डवेऽतोषयन्मृडम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācabāṇaḥ putra-śata-jyeṣṭhobaler āsīn mahātmanaḥyena vāmana-rūpāyaharaye ’dāyi medinī
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Bāṇa was the oldest of the hundred sons fathered by the great saint Bali Mahārāja, who gave the whole earth in charity to Lord Hari when He appeared as Vāmanadeva. Bāṇāsura, born from Bali’s semen, became a great devotee of Lord Śiva. His behavior was always respectable, and he was generous, intelligent, truthful and firm in his vows. The beautiful city of Śoṇitapura was under his dominion. Because Lord Śiva had favored him, the very demigods waited on Bāṇāsura like menial servants. Once, when Śiva was dancing his tāṇḍava-nṛtya, Bāṇa especially satisfied the lord by playing a musical accompaniment with his one thousand arms.

verses.sanskrit: भगवान् सर्वभूतेश: शरण्यो भक्तवत्सल: ।वरेण छन्दयामास स तं वव्रे पुराधिपम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavān sarva-bhūteśaḥśaraṇyo bhakta-vatsalaḥvareṇa chandayām āsasa taṁ vavre purādhipam
verses.text: The lord and master of all created beings, the compassionate refuge of his devotees, gladdened Bāṇāsura by offering him the benediction of his choice. Bāṇa chose to have him, Lord Śiva, as the guardian of his city.

verses.sanskrit: स एकदाह गिरिशं पार्श्वस्थं वीर्यदुर्मद: ।किरीटेनार्कवर्णेन संस्पृशंस्तत्पदाम्बुजम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa ekadāha giriśaṁpārśva-sthaṁ vīrya-durmadaḥkirīṭenārka-varṇenasaṁspṛśaṁs tat-padāmbujam
verses.text: Bāṇāsura was intoxicated with his strength. One day, when Lord Śiva was standing beside him, Bāṇāsura touched the lord’s lotus feet with his helmet, which shone like the sun, and spoke to him as follows.

verses.sanskrit: नमस्ये त्वां महादेव लोकानां गुरुमीश्वरम् ।पुंसामपूर्णकामानां कामपूरामराङ्‍‍घ्रिपम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: namasye tvāṁ mahā-devalokānāṁ gurum īśvarampuṁsām apūrṇa-kāmānāṁkāma-pūrāmarāṅghripam
verses.text: [Bāṇāsura said:] O Lord Mahādeva, I bow down to you, the spiritual master and controller of the worlds. You are like the heavenly tree that fulfills the desires of those whose desires are unfulfilled.

verses.sanskrit: दो:सहस्रं त्वया दत्तं परं भाराय मेऽभवत् ।त्रिलोक्यां प्रतियोद्धारं न लभे त्वद‍ृते समम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: doḥ-sahasraṁ tvayā dattaṁparaṁ bhārāya me ’bhavattri-lokyāṁ pratiyoddhāraṁna labhe tvad ṛte samam
verses.text: These one thousand arms you bestowed upon me have become merely a heavy burden. Besides you, I find no one in the three worlds worthy to fight.

verses.sanskrit: कण्डूत्या निभृतैर्दोर्भिर्युयुत्सुर्दिग्गजानहम् ।आद्यायां चूर्णयन्नद्रीन् भीतास्तेऽपि प्रदुद्रुवु: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaṇḍūtyā nibhṛtair dorbhiryuyutsur dig-gajān ahamādyāyāṁ cūrṇayann adrīnbhītās te ’pi pradudruvuḥ
verses.text: Eager to fight with the elephants who rule the directions, O primeval lord, I went forth, pulverizing mountains with my arms, which were itching for battle. But even those great elephants fled in fear.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा भगवान् क्रुद्ध: केतुस्ते भज्यते यदा ।त्वद्दर्पघ्नं भवेन्मूढ संयुगं मत्समेन ते ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tac chrutvā bhagavān kruddhaḥketus te bhajyate yadātvad-darpa-ghnaṁ bhaven mūḍhasaṁyugaṁ mat-samena te
verses.text: Hearing this, Lord Śiva became angry and replied, “Your flag will be broken, fool, when you have done battle with one who is my equal. That fight will vanquish your conceit.”

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्त: कुमतिर्हृष्ट: स्वगृहं प्राविशन्नृप ।प्रतीक्षन् गिरिशादेशं स्ववीर्यनशनं कुधी: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktaḥ kumatir hṛṣṭaḥsva-gṛhaṁ prāviśan nṛpapratīkṣan giriśādeśaṁsva-vīrya-naśanam kudhīḥ
verses.text: Thus advised, unintelligent Bāṇāsura was delighted. The fool then went home, O King, to wait for that which Lord Giriśa had predicted: the destruction of his prowess.

verses.sanskrit: तस्योषा नाम दुहिता स्वप्ने प्राद्युम्निना रतिम् ।कन्यालभत कान्तेन प्रागद‍ृष्टश्रुतेन सा ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyoṣā nāma duhitāsvapne prādyumninā ratimkanyālabhata kāntenaprāg adṛṣṭa-śrutena sā
verses.text: In a dream Bāṇa’s daughter, the maiden Ūṣā, had an amorous encounter with the son of Pradyumna, though she had never before seen or heard of her lover.

verses.sanskrit: सा तत्र तमपश्यन्ती क्व‍ासि कान्तेति वादिनी ।सखीनां मध्य उत्तस्थौ विह्वला व्रीडिता भृशम् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā tatra tam apaśyantīkvāsi kānteti vādinīsakhīnāṁ madhya uttasthauvihvalā vrīḍitā bhṛśam
verses.text: Losing sight of Him in her dream, Ūṣā suddenly sat up in the midst of her girlfriends, crying out “Where are You, my lover?” She was greatly disturbed and embarrassed.

verses.sanskrit: बाणस्य मन्त्री कुम्भाण्डश्चित्रलेखा च तत्सुता ।सख्यपृच्छत् सखीमूषां कौतूहलसमन्विता ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: bāṇasya mantrī kumbhāṇḍaścitralekhā ca tat-sutāsakhy apṛcchat sakhīm ūṣāṁkautūhala-samanvitā
verses.text: Bāṇāsura had a minister named Kumbhāṇḍa, whose daughter was Citralekhā. A companion of Ūṣā’s, she was filled with curiosity, and thus she inquired from her friend.

verses.sanskrit: कं त्वं मृगयसे सुभ्रु कीद‍ृशस्ते मनोरथ: ।हस्तग्राहं न तेऽद्यापि राजपुत्र्युपलक्षये ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaṁ tvaṁ mṛgayase su-bhrukīdṛśas te manorathaḥhasta-grāhaṁ na te ’dyāpirāja-putry upalakṣaye
verses.text: [Citralekhā said:] Who are you searching for, O fine-browed one? What is this hankering you’re feeling? Until now, O princess, I haven’t seen any man take your hand in marriage.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट: कश्चिन्नर: स्वप्ने श्याम: कमललोचन: ।पीतवासा बृहद्ब‍ाहुर्योषितां हृदयंगम: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭaḥ kaścin naraḥ svapneśyāmaḥ kamala-locanaḥpīta-vāsā bṛhad-bāhuryoṣitāṁ hṛdayaṁ-gamaḥ
verses.text: [Ūṣā said:] In my dream I saw a certain man who had a darkblue complexion, lotus eyes, yellow garments and mighty arms. He was the kind who touches women’s hearts.

verses.sanskrit: तमहं मृगये कान्तं पाययित्वाधरं मधु ।क्व‍ापि यात: स्पृहयतीं क्षिप्‍त्‍वा मां वृजिनार्णवे ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam ahaṁ mṛgaye kāntaṁpāyayitvādharaṁ madhukvāpi yātaḥ spṛhayatīṁkṣiptvā māṁ vṛjinārṇave
verses.text: It is that lover I search for. After making me drink the honey of His lips, He has gone elsewhere, and thus He has thrown me, hankering fervently for Him, into the ocean of distress.

verses.sanskrit: चित्रलेखोवाचव्यसनं तेऽपकर्षामि त्रिलोक्यां यदि भाव्यते ।तमानेष्ये नरं यस्ते मनोहर्ता तमादिश ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: citralekhovācavyasanaṁ te ’pakarṣāmitri-lokyāṁ yadi bhāvyatetam āneṣye varaṁ yas temano-hartā tam ādiśa
verses.text: Citralekhā said: I will remove your distress. If He is to be found anywhere in the three worlds, I will bring this future husband of yours who has stolen your heart. Please show me who He is.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा देवगन्धर्वसिद्धचारणपन्नगान् ।दैत्यविद्याधरान् यक्षान् मनुजांश्च यथालिखत् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktvā deva-gandharvasiddha-cāraṇa-pannagāndaitya-vidyādharān yakṣānmanujāṁś ca yathālikhat
verses.text: Saying this, Citralekhā proceeded to draw accurate pictures of various demigods, Gandharvas, Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Pannagas, Daityas, Vidyādharas, Yakṣas and humans.

verses.sanskrit: मनुजेषु च सा वृष्णीन् शूरमानकदुन्दुभिम् ।व्यलिखद् रामकृष्णौ च प्रद्युम्नं वीक्ष्य लज्जिता ॥ १८ ॥अनिरुद्धं विलिखितं वीक्ष्योषावाङ्‍मुखी ह्रिया ।सोऽसावसाविति प्राह स्मयमाना महीपते ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: manujeṣu ca sā vṛṣnīnśūram ānakadundubhimvyalikhad rāma-kṛṣṇau capradyumnaṁ vīkṣya lajjitā
verses.text: O King, among the humans, Citralekhā drew pictures of the Vṛṣṇis, including Śūrasena, Ānakadundubhi, Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa. When Ūṣā saw the picture of Pradyumna she became bashful, and when she saw Aniruddha’s picture she bent her head down in embarrassment. Smiling, she exclaimed, “He’s the one! It’s Him!”

verses.sanskrit: चित्रलेखा तमाज्ञाय पौत्रं कृष्णस्य योगिनी ।ययौ विहायसा राजन् द्वारकां कृष्णपालिताम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: citralekhā tam ājñāyapautraṁ kṛṣṇasya yoginīyayau vihāyasā rājandvārakāṁ kṛṣṇa-pālitām
verses.text: Citralekhā, endowed with mystic powers, recognized Him as Kṛṣṇa’s grandson [Aniruddha]. My dear King, she then traveled by the mystic skyway to Dvārakā, the city under Lord Kṛṣṇa’s protection.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र सुप्तं सुपर्यङ्के प्राद्युम्निं योगमास्थिता ।गृहीत्वा शोणितपुरं सख्यै प्रियमदर्शयत् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra suptaṁ su-paryaṅkeprādyumniṁ yogam āsthitāgṛhītvā śoṇita-puraṁsakhyai priyam adarśayat
verses.text: There she found Pradyumna’s son Aniruddha sleeping upon a fine bed. With her yogic power she took Him away to Śoṇitapura, where she presented her girlfriend Ūṣā with her beloved.

verses.sanskrit: सा च तं सुन्दरवरं विलोक्य मुदितानना ।दुष्प्रेक्ष्ये स्वगृहे पुम्भी रेमे प्राद्युम्निना समम् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā ca taṁ sundara-varaṁvilokya muditānanāduṣprekṣye sva-gṛhe pumbhīreme prādyumninā samam
verses.text: When Ūṣā beheld Him, the most beautiful of men, her face lit up with joy. She took the son of Pradyumna to her private quarters, which men were forbidden even to see, and there enjoyed with Him.

verses.sanskrit: परार्ध्यवास:स्रग्गन्धधूपदीपासनादिभि: ।पानभोजनभक्ष्यैश्च वाक्यै: शुश्रूषणार्चित: ॥ २३ ॥गूढ: कन्यापुरे शश्वत्प्रवृद्धस्‍नेहया तया ।नाहर्गणान् स बुबुधे ऊषयापहृतेन्द्रिय: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: parārdhya-vāsaḥ-srag-gandha-dhūpa-dīpāsanādibhiḥpāna-bhojana-bhakṣyaiś cavākyaiḥ śuśrūṣaṇārcitaḥ
verses.text: Ūṣā worshiped Aniruddha with faithful service, offering Him priceless garments, along with garlands, fragrances, incense, lamps, sitting places and so on. She also offered Him beverages, all types of food, and sweet words. As He thus remained hidden in the young ladies’ quarters, Aniruddha did not notice the passing of the days, for His senses were captivated by Ūṣā, whose affection for Him ever increased.

verses.sanskrit: तां तथा यदुवीरेण भुज्यमानां हतव्रताम् ।हेतुभिर्लक्षयां चक्रुरापृईतां दुरवच्छदै: ॥ २५ ॥भटा आवेदयां चक्रू राजंस्ते दुहितुर्वयम् ।विचेष्टितं लक्षयाम कन्याया: कुलदूषणम् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁ tathā yadu-vīreṇabhujyamānāṁ hata-vratāmhetubhir lakṣayāṁ cakrurāpṛītāṁ duravacchadaiḥ
verses.text: The female guards eventually noticed unmistakable symptoms of romantic involvement in Ūṣā, who, having broken her maiden vow, was being enjoyed by the Yadu hero and showing signs of conjugal happiness. The guards went to Bāṇāsura and told him, “O King, we have detected in your daughter the kind of improper behavior that spoils the reputation of a young girl’s family.

verses.sanskrit: अनपायिभिरस्माभिर्गुप्तायाश्च गृहे प्रभो ।कन्याया दूषणं पुम्भिर्दुष्प्रेक्ष्याया न विद्महे ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: anapāyibhir asmābhirguptāyāś ca gṛhe prabhokanyāyā dūṣaṇaṁ pumbhirduṣprekṣyāyā na vidmahe
verses.text: “We have been carefully watching over her, never leaving our posts, O master, so we cannot understand how this maiden, whom no man can even see, has been corrupted within the palace.”

verses.sanskrit: तत: प्रव्यथितो बाणो दुहितु: श्रुतदूषण: ।त्वरित: कन्यकागारं प्राप्तोऽद्राक्षीद् यदूद्वहम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ pravyathito bāṇoduhituḥ śruta-dūṣaṇaḥtvaritaḥ kanyakāgāraṁprāpto ’drākṣīd yadūdvaham
verses.text: Very agitated to hear of his daughter’s corruption, Bāṇāsura rushed at once to the maidens’ quarters. There he saw the pride of the Yadus, Aniruddha.

verses.sanskrit: कामात्मजं तं भुवनैकसुन्दरंश्यामं पिशङ्गाम्बरमम्बुजेक्षणम् ।बृहद्भ‍ुजं कुण्डलकुन्तलत्विषास्मितावलोकेन च मण्डिताननम् ॥ २९ ॥दीव्यन्तमक्षै: प्रिययाभिनृम्णयातदङ्गसङ्गस्तनकुङ्कुमस्रजम् ।बाह्वोर्दधानं मधुमल्लिकाश्रितांतस्याग्र आसीनमवेक्ष्य विस्मित: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: kāmātmajaṁ taṁ bhuvanaika-sundaraṁśyāmaṁ piśaṅgāmbaram ambujekṣaṇambṛhad-bhujaṁ kuṇḍala-kuntala-tviṣāsmitāvalokena ca maṇḍitānanam
verses.text: Bāṇāsura saw before him Cupid’s own son, possessed of unrivaled beauty, with dark-blue complexion, yellow garments, lotus eyes and formidable arms. His face was adorned with effulgent earrings and hair, and also with smiling glances. As He sat opposite His most auspicious lover, playing with her at dice, there hung between His arms a garland of spring jasmines that had been smeared with kuṅkuma powder from her breasts when He had embraced her. Bāṇāsura was astonished to see all this.

verses.sanskrit: स तं प्रविष्टं वृतमाततायिभि-र्भटैरनीकैरवलोक्य माधव: ।उद्यम्य मौर्वं परिघं व्यवस्थितोयथान्तको दण्डधरो जिघांसया ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa taṁ praviṣṭaṁ vṛtam ātatāyibhirbhaṭair anīkair avalokya mādhavaḥudyamya maurvaṁ parighaṁ vyavasthitoyathāntako daṇḍa-dharo jighāṁsayā
verses.text: Seeing Bāṇāsura enter with many armed guards, Aniruddha raised His iron club and stood resolute, ready to strike anyone who attacked Him. He resembled death personified holding his rod of punishment.

verses.sanskrit: जिघृक्षया तान् परित: प्रसर्पत:शुनो यथा शूकरयूथपोऽहनत् ।ते हन्यमाना भवनाद् विनिर्गतानिर्भिन्नमूर्धोरुभुजा: प्रदुद्रुवु: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: jighṛkṣayā tān paritaḥ prasarpataḥśuno yathā śūkara-yūthapo ’hanatte hanyamānā bhavanād vinirgatānirbhinna-mūrdhoru-bhujāḥ pradudruvuḥ
verses.text: As the guards converged on Him from all sides, trying to capture Him, Aniruddha struck them just as the leader of a pack of boars strikes back at dogs. Hit by His blows, the guards fled the palace, running for their lives with shattered heads, thighs and arms.

verses.sanskrit: तं नागपाशैर्बलिनन्दनो बलीघ्नन्तं स्वसैन्यं कुपितो बबन्ध ह ।ऊषा भृशं शोकविषादविह्वलाबद्धं निशम्याश्रुकलाक्ष्यरौत्सीत् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ nāga-pāśair bali-nandano balīghnantaṁ sva-sainyaṁ kupito babandha haūṣā bhṛśaṁ śoka-viṣāda-vihvalābaddhaṁ niśamyāśru-kalākṣy arautsīt
verses.text: But even as Aniruddha was striking down the army of Bāṇa, that powerful son of Bali angrily caught Him with the mystic nāga-pāśa ropes. When Ūṣā heard of Aniruddha’s capture, she was overwhelmed with grief and depression; her eyes filled with tears, and she wept.
